scope: Authored Blender viewport material parity on the exact Geometry Nodes mesh. The procedural filament surface does not slice the bracket and is not a G-code toolpath.

geometry.evaluationContext: The source generator is evaluated at an identity transform so Relative Object Info matches parity_sweep.py and the browser local-space preview. Appending a Realize Instances modifier changes this dependency-sensitive graph and is intentionally skipped.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity use renderer-specific units. The recorded scales align mean surface exposure under the shared normalized rig; they do not claim equal physical power.

recoveredGraph.color: POINT-domain col attribute written from the authored filament color input
recoveredGraph.frontColor: the authored Mix Color branch resolves to col for the default equal-hue inputs
recoveredGraph.backColor: Geometry Backfacing selects the authored darker branch

interpretation: The authoritative long bracket, detached fastener, complete material assignment, POINT col branch, Generated-coordinate layer bands, distorted Wave texture, Backfacing branch, and Bump parameters are represented in the browser. Exact geometry and subpixel-aligned silhouettes prove that both captures use the same dependency-relative local-space result. Exposure is aligned to a 0.0014 mean-luminance delta. Remaining pixel and black-occupancy error is concentrated in the high-frequency Wave/Bump response, where Eevee and WebGL sample, filter, and light the bands differently.
